system_prompt: You are participating in a research study about how AI models use and attribute information sources in AI-for-software-engineering questions. Answer carefully. Do not invent papers, URLs, DOIs, or venue facts. If uncertain, say so.
user_prompt:
Identify 10 influential papers for AI-assisted software engineering, including older software engineering research and recent LLM/code-generation work. Include title, authors, venue, year, URL or DOI if known, and a short impact summary.

Return JSON with:
- ranked_items: list of objects with rank, title, authors, venue, year, justification
- references: list of objects with title, authors, venue, year, url, doi, arxiv_id, source_type, claimed_purpose
- uncertainty_notes
response_text:
The user is asking for 10 influential papers in AI-assisted software engineering, spanning older SE research and recent LLM/code-generation work. They want specific metadata (title, authors, venue, year, URL/DOI) and a short impact summary. They want JSON output with specific structure.

I need to be careful: I should not invent papers, URLs, DOIs, or venue facts. If uncertain, I should say so. I have knowledge of many influential papers in this space up to my knowledge cutoff (2024-04). I can list well-known papers with high confidence.

Let me think of 10 influential papers:

1. "Deep Code Search" (Gu et al., ICSE 2018) - early deep learning for code search
2. "CodeBERT: A Pre-Trained Model for Programming and Natural Languages" (Feng et al., EMNLP 2020) - influential pre-trained model for code
3. "GraphCodeBERT: Pre-training Code Representations with Data Flow" (Guo et al., ICLR 2021) - improved code representation
4. "Codex: Evaluating Large Language Models Trained on Code" (Chen et al., 2021, arXiv) - the Codex paper
5. "GitHub Copilot: AI Pair Programmer" (not a paper per se, but there's a technical report) - maybe "GitHub Copilot: A Technical Preview" or similar
6. "AlphaCode: Competition-Level Code Generation with AlphaCode" (Li et al., 2022, Science) - DeepMind's AlphaCode
7. "StarCoder: A State-of-the-Art LLM for Code" (Li et al., 2023, arXiv) - BigCode's StarCoder
8. "CodeLlama: Open Foundation Models for Code" (Rozière et al., 2023, arXiv) - Meta's CodeLlama
9. "Program Synthesis with Large Language Models" (Austin et al., 2021, arXiv) - early LLM for program synthesis
10. "Neural Program Synthesis from Diverse Demonstration Videos" (not sure) - maybe "Learning to Generate Code from Natural Language" (Ling et al., 2016) - early seq2seq for
